stories.title: Scaffold NestJS project, install dependencies, remove defaults
stories.goal: Produce a bootable NestJS backend at backend/ with all runtime/dev dependencies installed, the default AppController/AppService removed, and a spec that proves AppModule compiles.

acceptanceCriteria.text: backend/package.json has all required runtime dependencies
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && node -e "const p=require('./package.json'); const r=['@nestjs/core','@nestjs/common','@nestjs/typeorm','typeorm','better-sqlite3','pg','@nestjs/config','class-validator','class-transformer','@nestjs/schedule']; const m=r.filter(d=>!p.dependencies[d]); if(m.length){console.error('missing',m);process.exit(1)}"

acceptanceCriteria.text: default AppController and AppService removed (no files, no registrations)
acceptanceCriteria.check: ! test -f backend/src/app.controller.ts && ! test -f backend/src/app.service.ts && ! test -f backend/src/app.controller.spec.ts && ! test -f backend/src/app.service.spec.ts

stories.notes: Use `npx @nestjs/cli new backend --package-manager pnpm --skip-git --strict` then surgically remove AppController/AppService. The CLI also generates app.controller.spec.ts and app.service.spec.ts — remove those too. The spec file app.module.spec.ts must import AppModule and assert it is a class decorated with @Module. Do NOT add TypeORM, ConfigModule, or any feature module here — those belong to later stories.

stories.title: Configure @shared/* path alias in tsconfig
stories.goal: Wire the @shared/* path alias so backend source can import from the repo-root shared/interfaces/ directory. Prove it with a spec that imports ApiResponse from @shared/interfaces.

regions.reason: compilerOptions.paths — add @shared/* mapping to ../shared/*

acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && ! jq -e '.exclude | index("../shared") // false' tsconfig.build.json >/dev/null

acceptanceCriteria.text: backend still builds with alias resolved

stories.notes: Per resolution: do NOT exclude ../shared from tsconfig.build.json. TypeScript needs to resolve @shared/* types at compile time; they are type-only imports and produce no output in dist/.

stories.title: TypeORM dual-database config (SQLite/Postgres) + AppModule wiring
stories.goal: Create getTypeOrmConfig(configService) that returns Postgres config when DATABASE_URL is set, SQLite config otherwise. Register via TypeOrmModule.forRootAsync + ConfigModule.forRoot in AppModule. SQLite config must mkdirSync('data',{recursive:true}) before returning. Prove all branches with a table-driven spec.

regions.reason: imports block — add ConfigModule and TypeOrmModule imports
@Module decorator imports array — register ConfigModule.forRoot() and TypeOrmModule.forRootAsync({useFactory: getTypeOrmConfig, inject: [ConfigService]})

acceptanceCriteria.text: config factory calls mkdirSync('data', {recursive:true}) for sqlite path
typeorm.config.spec.ts passes (table-driven: DATABASE_URL set → postgres, unset → sqlite)

acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && grep -q "TypeOrmModule.forRootAsync" src/app.module.ts && grep -q "ConfigModule.forRoot" src/app.module.ts

stories.notes: Per resolution: the glob __dirname + '/../**/*.entity{.ts,.js}' is correct because at runtime typeorm.config.ts compiles to dist/config/, so __dirname = dist/config/, ../ = dist/, glob matches dist/**/*.entity.js. Per resolution: mkdirSync('data', {recursive:true}) MUST be called in the factory before returning the sqlite config — better-sqlite3 won't create the parent dir. The spec must mock ConfigService.get('DATABASE_URL') and assert both branches. Do NOT use synchronize:true in the postgres branch.

Register all 6 in AppModule. Prove registration with a spec.

Controllers and services are EMPTY SCAFFOLDS in this story — just the class with @Controller()/@Injectable() decorator and an empty body. No endpoints, no methods. They get implemented in Epic 3 (query API). The modules.spec.ts must assert each module class exists, is decorated with @Module, and (where applicable) registers its controller/provider.

stories.notes: Per resolution: perCapita stored as integer cents/person (not dollars). Per resolution: recoveryRatio is dimensionless float — the cents rule does NOT apply. Per resolution: GeoSpendingSnapshot unique constraint includes nullable agencyId; SQLite allows multiple NULL agencyId rows — accept this, dedupe at app level in Epic 2. Use the standard @Unique decorator. Entities use @Column({ type: 'integer' }) for cents fields in sqlite. Use TypeORM metadata in the spec (getMetadataArgsStorage) to assert columns and unique constraints declaratively — do NOT spin up a database for unit tests.

stories.title: Response wrapper interceptor + @Paginated() decorator
stories.goal: Create ResponseWrapperInterceptor that wraps paginated list responses as ApiResponse<T[]> = { data, meta: { total, page, pageSize } } and leaves single-item responses unwrapped. Opt-in via @Paginated() decorator. Import ApiResponse<T> from @shared/interfaces — do NOT duplicate it.

acceptanceCriteria.text: interceptor spec passes (table-driven: list wraps, single item passes through, non-paginated route unaffected)

stories.notes: Per resolution: do NOT create common/api-response.interface.ts. ApiResponse<T> already lives in @shared/interfaces/index.ts — import it from there. The interceptor checks for the @Paginated() metadata via reflect-metadata; if present and the response is an array, wrap as {data, meta:{total:arr.length, page, pageSize}}. If absent or response is not an array, pass through unchanged. The spec must mock ExecutionContext and the next handler, run the interceptor with a table of {input shape, hasPaginated, expected output shape}.

stories.title: End-to-end verification of Epic 1
stories.goal: Verify the full Epic 1 surface with one e2e spec that boots the NestJS app, hits /health, and asserts all 5 entity tables exist in the sqlite file. No new production code — only the spec and any small wiring fixes surfaced by the e2e run.

acceptanceCriteria.text: all backend unit specs pass (pnpm test)
no AppController or AppService anywhere in backend/src

stories.notes: The e2e spec uses Test.createTestingModule({ imports: [AppModule] }).compile() then app.getHttpServer() with supertest to GET /health. Then it queries the sqlite file (or DataSource.query) to assert the 5 entity tables exist. If the e2e surfaces a wiring gap (missing module registration, broken import path), fix it inside the files already in scope of prior stories — do NOT create new files. This story's scope is intentionally just the e2e spec; the model may edit previously-committed files only to close wiring gaps surfaced by the e2e.

stories.title: UsaSpendingService — Fetch/Transform/Return deep module
stories.goal: Implement UsaSpendingService as ONE deep module that fetches from USASpending.gov, transforms raw rows into cents-integers, and returns typed entity-shaped objects. Methods: fetchAgencies(), fetchSpendingByAgency(agency, fiscalYear), fetchGeoSnapshots(agency, fiscalYear, scope), fetchDisasterSpending(defGroup). Private retry wrapper (3 attempts, exponential backoff). No upsert here — SyncService owns persistence.

retry logic present (3 attempts, exponential backoff)

stories.notes: Deep module per AGENTS.md Skill 3: ONE file, top-to-bottom legible. The Fetch (HTTP), Transform (raw dollars → cents), and Return (typed) steps stay together. Use global fetch() — no axios dependency. Mock fetch in the spec via jest.spyOn(global, 'fetch'). The spec testTable must cover: (a) raw dollar field 1234.56 → obligatedAmount 123456 cents, (b) pagination handled across multiple pages, (c) HTTP 500 then 200 → retry succeeds, (d) HTTP 500 thrice → throws after 3 attempts. Do NOT call the real USASpending API in unit tests — all fetches mocked.

stories.title: Recovery ratio calculator — pure module with edge cases
stories.goal: Implement a pure function computeRecoveryRatio(femaCents, fedCents) that returns DisasterRecoveryRatio shape. Handles 4 cases: both zero → 1.0, fema zero fed positive → Infinity, fema positive fed zero → 0, both positive → fed/fema. Table-driven spec covers all 4 + boundary values.

acceptanceCriteria.text: recovery-ratio.spec.ts passes (table-driven across 4 edge cases + boundaries)
function is pure (no service injection, no side effects)

stories.notes: Edge cases per epic-02 spec: both 0 → 1.0 (neutral); fema 0 fed >0 → Infinity (well-resourced); fema >0 fed 0 → 0 (complete gap); both >0 → fed/fema. Spec testTable must include boundary values like {fema:1, fed:Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER} to verify no overflow. This is a pure helper — not @Injectable — because it has no dependencies. SyncService in E2-S05 imports and calls it.

stories.notes: Single deep module per AGENTS.md Skill 3 — one ApiService, one method per use case, HttpClient injected, base URL from environment.apiUrl. provideHttpClient is NOT wired in this story (deferred to E4-S04); tests use provideHttpClientTesting + HttpTestingController, so do NOT edit app.config.ts. CRITICAL RESPONSE SHAPES — the backend has NO @Paginated decorator, so its ResponseWrapperInterceptor passes everything through RAW. ONLY GET /agencies returns a wrapped {data,meta} object (the service pre-wraps it); every other endpoint returns a raw array or raw object. So ONLY getAgencies maps r.data; the other 8 methods return the body directly (r). Per-method contract: getAgencies() GET /agencies -> {data,meta}; map r.data; the item shape is {id,name,totalCents} (NOT the shared Agency shape — backend drift), so define a local AgencyWithTotal interface. getAgencySummary(id) GET /agencies/:id/summary -> raw AgencySummary | null; return r. getAgencySpotlight(id) GET /agencies/:id/spotlight -> raw SpendingRecord[] | null; return r. getGeographyStates(params) GET /geography/states with HttpParams {fiscalYear?,agencyId?,scope?} -> raw GeoSpendingSnapshot[]; return r. getGeographyState(code) GET /geography/state/:code -> raw GeoSpendingSnapshot[]; return r. getDisasterOverview() GET /disaster/overview -> raw ARRAY DisasterOverview[] (note: array, not a single object); return r. getDisasterStates(params) GET /disaster/states with HttpParams {defGroup?,fiscalYear?} -> raw DisasterFundingRecord[]; return r. getDisasterRecoveryRatios(params) GET /disaster/recovery-ratios with HttpParams {fiscalYear?} -> raw DisasterRecoveryRatio[]; return r. getDisasterState(code) GET /disaster/state/:code -> raw state-profile object (no matching @shared/interfaces type — define a local DisasterStateProfile interface). Import matching types from @shared/interfaces (GeoSpendingSnapshot, DisasterRecoveryRatio, SpendingRecord, AgencySummary, DisasterOverview, ApiResponse). Build HttpParams skipping undefined values. catchError fallbacks: of([] as T[]) for list methods, of(null) for single/null methods. Spec (api.service.spec.ts) TABLE-DRIVEN: one testTable row per method {name, methodName, args, expectedUrl, mockBody, expected}; TestBed with provideHttpClientTesting, inject HttpTestingController; for each row call the method, expectOne(expectedUrl), flush mockBody (the REAL backend shape per the contract above), assert the emitted value equals expected (post-map: r.data for getAgencies, the body for the rest). No comments in code.

stories.title: LoadingService + LoadingInterceptor + ErrorInterceptor
stories.goal: LoadingService exposes `loading$: WritableSignal<boolean>`. LoadingInterceptor increments/decrements a counter on every HTTP request, sets loading$ true when >0. ErrorInterceptor catches non-2xx, shows mat-snack-bar with the error message, does NOT retry (re-throws so downstream catchError fallbacks still fire). Wire both via provideHttpClient(withInterceptors([loadingInterceptor, errorInterceptor])) in app.config.ts, and add provideAnimationsAsync() so MatSnackBar works at runtime.

acceptanceCriteria.text: app.config.ts registers both interceptors via withInterceptors([...])
app.config.ts provides animations (MatSnackBar requires it at runtime)

stories.notes: Angular 20. CRITICAL: providers go in app.config.ts, NOT main.ts. main.ts just does bootstrapApplication(App, appConfig) — do NOT edit it. In app.config.ts add to the EXISTING providers array: provideHttpClient(withInterceptors([loadingInterceptor, errorInterceptor])) AND provideAnimationsAsync() (import from @angular/platform-browser/animations/async — MatSnackBar needs animations at runtime; without it, opening a snack-bar throws). Keep the existing provideRouter(routes), provideBrowserGlobalErrorListeners(), and provideZoneChangeDetection. Interceptors are FUNCTIONAL (HttpInterceptorFn from @angular/common/http), NOT classes. LoadingService (@Injectable({providedIn:'root'})): a private counter field, increment()/decrement() methods, and readonly loading$ = signal(false) as a WritableSignal<boolean> (signal from @angular/core). decrement clamps counter at >=0; both methods call loading$.set(this.counter > 0). Only loading$ and increment/decrement are public. LoadingInterceptor (HttpInterceptorFn): inject(LoadingService), call increment() before forwarding, return next(req).pipe(finalize(() => loadingSvc.decrement())) so the counter decrements on BOTH success and error. ErrorInterceptor (HttpInterceptorFn): inject(MatSnackBar), return next(req).pipe(catchError((error: HttpErrorResponse) => { const msg = error.status ? error.status + ': ' + error.message : 'Network error'; snackBar.open(msg, 'Dismiss', { duration: 5000 }); return throwError(() => error); })) — RE-THROW after showing the snack-bar so the ApiService catchError fallbacks still fire (the service still returns [] / null to its subscribers); do NOT swallow. Specs: loading.service.spec.ts TABLE-DRIVEN over a testTable of {operations, expectedLoading} rows asserting loading$() after increment/decrement sequences. interceptors.spec.ts: TestBed with provideHttpClient(withInterceptors([loadingInterceptor, errorInterceptor])) + provideHttpClientTesting, HttpTestingController; for LoadingInterceptor assert the LoadingService counter/loading$ toggles around a flushed request; for ErrorInterceptor spy on MatSnackBar.open (inject MatSnackBar, jest.spyOn) and assert open() is called with the message AND the error re-throws (subscribe with error handler). No comments in code.

  Also guard: if either input is NaN, return 0.
  Update recovery-ratio.spec.ts: the test row for femaCents=0 fedCents>0 should expect 0, not Infinity.

BUG 2: upsertBy causes UNIQUE constraint failures
  File: sync.service.ts
  Current: findOne(match) -> save(entity). When id is undefined, TypeORM tries INSERT. If the record already exists (race, re-sync, stale data), INSERT fails with UNIQUE constraint.
  Error: SqliteError: UNIQUE constraint failed: agency.toptierCode
  Fix: replace upsertBy with TypeORM's repo.upsert() method:
    await repo.upsert(entity, conflictColumns);
  For agencies: conflict on ['toptierCode']
  For spending records: conflict on ['agencyId','fiscalYear','quarter','awardTypeLabel']
  For geo snapshots: conflict on ['stateCode','fiscalYear','agencyId','scope']
  For disaster records: conflict on ['defGroup','stateCode']
  Note: SQLite supports ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE. TypeORM's upsert() uses this internally.
  If repo.upsert() is unavailable in this TypeORM version, use createQueryBuilder()
    .insert().into(Entity).values(entity)
    .orUpdate(['columnName'], ['conflictColumn'])
    .execute();

BUG 3: transformGeoRows hardcodes fiscalYear
  File: usa-spending.service.ts
  Current: transformGeoRows sets fiscalYear: 0 on every snapshot.
  Fix: pass fiscalYear as a third parameter:
    transformGeoRows(rows, scope, fiscalYear)
  Callers: fetchSpendingByAgency knows params.fiscalYear.
           fetchGeoSnapshots knows params.fiscalYear.
           fetchDisasterSpending uses FY2024 (hardcoded in the POST body).

ROOT-CAUSE DETAIL: (1) defGroup label/code mismatch - frontend sent human labels (COVID-19) but DB stores USASpending DEF codes (L); the other 4 tabs (Hurricane/Wildfire/Infrastructure/General) remain empty pending a multi-group sync + full DEF-code map. (2) The recovery ratio (fed DEF / FEMA obligated) was conceptually mismatched - USASpending DEF spending includes non-FEMA agencies (trillions for COVID), while FEMA Public Assistance is a narrow physical-disaster-repair program (COVID PA nationwide is -$178M); aligning disaster numbers cannot fix it because the two data sources measure different things. stories.title: Deploy prep: backend serves frontend (SPA) + API under /api + render.yaml
stories.goal: Make the app deployable as a single service. The Nest backend now serves the Angular static build (useStaticAssets + SPA fallback to index.html for non-API GET routes), all API routes are namespaced under /api (via setGlobalPrefix) to avoid collision with Angular client-side routes (/agencies, /geography, /disaster), and render.yaml defines the free-tier web service + Postgres for Render.com deploy. The frontend prod apiUrl is /api (relative, same-origin).

acceptanceCriteria.text: backend main.ts sets global prefix api (excluding health)
backend serves static assets with SPA fallback to index.html
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && grep -q 'useStaticAssets' src/main.ts && grep -q 'indexHtml' src/main.ts

acceptanceCriteria.text: render.yaml exists with a free-tier web service + database
acceptanceCriteria.check: test -f render.yaml && grep -q 'plan: free' render.yaml && grep -q 'databases:' render.yaml

stories.notes: Deploy architecture: single Render web service (free tier) running node backend/dist/backend/src/main.js which serves both the API (under /api) and the Angular SPA (static assets + index.html fallback). DATABASE_URL from Render Postgres (free tier, 90 days) switches TypeORM to postgres automatically (typeorm.config.ts). On first deploy the Postgres DB is empty; the Init migration (migrationsRun:true) creates the schema, then a manual POST /api/sync/agencies, /api/sync/geography, /api/sync/disaster backfill populates the data against the live USASpending/FEMA APIs. The SPA fallback middleware in main.ts serves index.html for any GET that is not /api, /health, or a file extension (so deep-links like /agencies/30 work). NestExpressApplication gives direct express access for useStaticAssets + the fallback. CORS origin is FRONTEND_URL or * (not strictly needed for same-origin deploy but kept for dev). No pi/build-agent run; harness=opencode.

stories.title: Modernize Angular components to functional/signal style (remove all lifecycle hooks)
stories.goal: Make the frontend a consistent Angular 20 teaching example. Every component loses ngOnInit/ngOnDestroy and constructor DI in favor of inject(), signal state (signal/toSignal/computed/input), and reactive pipelines (toObservable+switchMap). Behavior is preserved: the 132 originally-green tests stay green with identical expected values (only access changes x -> x()). The already-red dashboard suite (18 failing: missing getDisasterOverview mock + stale chart expectations) is fixed to green as part of this story by reconciling the spec to the component actual behavior.

acceptanceCriteria.text: no component implements OnInit/OnDestroy or defines ngOnInit/ngOnDestroy
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd frontend && ! grep -rqE "implements OnInit|implements OnDestroy|ngOnInit\(|ngOnDestroy\(" src/app --include="*.component.ts"

acceptanceCriteria.text: no component uses constructor(private ...) DI (inject() only)
no component uses @Input() decorator (input() signal inputs only)
no component tracks subscriptions manually (no Subscription field / unsubscribe)
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd frontend && ! grep -rqE ": Subscription|new Subscription|\.unsubscribe\(" src/app --include="*.component.ts"

acceptanceCriteria.text: modern signal APIs are present across components (toSignal/input/computed)
frontend full test suite green (150 tests, incl. the previously-red dashboard 18)

stories.notes: Refactor sweep of all 6 feature components to a uniform modern-functional Angular 20 style. Per component: BarChart @Input()->input(), getters->computed(); AgencyList one toSignal + drop OnInit; Dashboard toSignal per HTTP source + computed derived state; AgencySpotlight route param -> toSignal(toObservable(id).pipe(switchMap)) + helpers refactored to PURE static methods (computeInsight/buildTableData/computeBadge/computeAvailableYears/aggregateAwardTypesForRange) so computed signals call them and the spec unit-tests them directly; DisasterLens OnDestroy + 3 manual Subscriptions deleted, filter signals drive toSignal(toObservable(filters).pipe(switchMap)) pipelines; GeographicView imperative loadData()/processData() replaced by a reactive filter->computed pipeline with delta derived from primary+secondary signals. KEY TESTING LESSON: toSignal subscribes at CONSTRUCTION, so every spec that spies on the service must install the spy BEFORE TestBed.createComponent and create a fresh fixture per test (the old create-once-in-beforeEach + spyOn-in-it pattern leaks a real HTTP call through the unmocked service at construction). Templates updated to signal-call syntax (x -> x()). Dashboard fix: the suite was already red (18 failing) because the spec never mocked getDisasterOverview and expected a stale Other/sum-of-rest chart; reconciled spec to the richer component behavior (Disaster Spending bar = overview total). No existing green assertion weakened: 132/132 preserved, dashboard 18 red->green, total 150/150. Built by opencode/GLM-5.2 directly (harness=opencode), NOT via the pi tier ladder, per user request. Baseline before: 132 green / 18 red.

stories.title: Backend boundary hardening: dissolve phantom SpendingModule + real DTO/ValidationPipe boundary
stories.goal: Close the deep-architecture gap between AGENTS.md Skill 1 (ValidationPipe is the only parsing boundary) and the read controllers. (1) Delete the empty phantom SpendingModule/Controller/Service stubs that imply an ownership they do not exercise (SpendingRecord is already owned by AgenciesModule via forFeature). (2) Replace manual parseInt in DisasterController and implicit @Query coercion in GeographyController with validated Query DTOs so the global ValidationPipe (whitelist+forbidNonWhitelisted+transform) actually enforces types and ranges. Behavior is preserved for valid input; bad input now rejects at the boundary instead of silently coercing.

regions.reason: delete the SpendingModule import line.
delete the SpendingModule entry from the root @Module imports array.
delete the SpendingModule import.
delete the expect(SpendingModule).toBeDefined() assertion (and any sibling block referencing it).

acceptanceCriteria.text: phantom SpendingModule + its empty controller are removed
no manual number parsing remains in any controller (ValidationPipe is the boundary)
geography query DTO exists and enforces class-validator rules
disaster query DTO exists and enforces class-validator rules
backend full test suite green (excludes sync/usa-spending.service.spec.ts, tracked separately as E20-S01: its rate-limiter real-setTimeout drift is pre-existing and orthogonal to this story)

stories.notes: Deep-architecture cleanup (see AGENTS.md Skill 1 + Skill 3). SpendingModule is a shallow phantom: spending.module.ts is `export class SpendingModule {}` with an empty controller and service, while SpendingRecord is actually read via AgenciesService (@InjectRepository(SpendingRecord)). Dissolve the stubs; KEEP backend/src/spending/spending-record.entity.ts and backend/src/spending/dto/ (still used). DTO pattern: each read controller takes @Query() dto: XQueryDto (class-validator decorators @IsInt/@IsString/@IsOptional/@Min/@Max) instead of individual @Query string params. CRITICAL DETAIL: with transform:true the pipe coerces, but query params arrive as STRINGS — number fields in the DTO need @Type(() => Number) from class-transformer to coerce before @IsInt validates. Mirror the existing geography QueryStatesInput / disaster QueryStatesParams field shapes. GeographyController currently relies on implicit number coercion of @Query; DisasterController currently does manual parseInt(fiscalYear,10) — both become a single validated DTO. AgencySpotlightQueryDto already exists in backend/src/agencies/dto/ but is vestigial (not wired) — either wire it or add a new AgencyListQueryDto for the list endpoint and delete the vestigial one. Tests are table-driven (AGENTS.md Skill 2): for each DTO, rows of valid input (passes through) + bad input (non-int, out-of-range, unknown field) rejected by the pipe. Controller specs mock the service and assert the DTO is forwarded as the typed object. Do NOT touch the Sync module or any write pipeline — those are already exemplary deep modules.

stories.title: Unify response shape to raw everywhere: drop ApiResponse envelope + dead interceptor
stories.goal: Make the API contract one uniform shape (raw arrays/objects) end-to-end. Today only GET /api/agencies is wrapped in ApiResponse{data,meta} with a FAKE meta (total/age/pageSize always = data.length) — the other 7 endpoints already return raw and the frontend ApiService expects raw. The ResponseWrapperInterceptor in common/ is dead code: never registered (no APP_INTERCEPTOR), relies on an isPaginated reflector key nobody sets, and its own spec is the only consumer. Remove the envelope, delete the dead interceptor, and drop the now-unused ApiResponse interface from @shared/interfaces. Backend + frontend ship together so /api/agencies and getAgencies change shape in lockstep within one deploy.

acceptanceCriteria.text: dead ResponseWrapperInterceptor + its spec are removed
no ApiResponse reference remains anywhere (backend, frontend, shared)
agencies service no longer constructs an ApiResponse envelope
frontend getAgencies treats /api/agencies as a raw array (no .data unwrap)
backend full test suite + build green (excludes sync/usa-spending.service.spec.ts, tracked separately as E20-S01: its rate-limiter real-setTimeout drift is pre-existing and orthogonal to this story)
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && pnpm test --testPathIgnorePatterns=usa-spending && pnpm build

acceptanceCriteria.text: frontend full test suite + build green
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd frontend && pnpm test && pnpm build

stories.notes: Cross-stack contract change (BUILD-DEEP). AgenciesService.findAllWithTotals currently returns Promise<ApiResponse<{id,name,totalCents}[]>> building {data, meta:{total:data.length, page:1, pageSize:data.length}} — the meta is fake (no real pagination), pure ceremony. Change it to return the raw {id,name,totalCents}[] array directly; update AgenciesController list() return type to match. agencies.service.spec.ts currently asserts result.data and result.meta — rewrite those rows to assert the raw array (toStrictEqual the array), mirroring the findSummary/findSpotlight spec style already in that file. Delete backend/src/common/response-wrapper.interceptor.ts AND its spec (response-wrapper.interceptor.spec.ts) — confirmed never registered (grep APP_INTERCEPTOR = empty) and its isPaginated reflector key is set nowhere. Remove the ApiResponse interface from shared/interfaces/index.ts (repo-root file, stays in place per AGENTS.md) — it is unused after this. Frontend: api.service.ts getAgencies currently does http.get<{data: AgencyWithTotal[]}>(...).pipe(map(r => r.data)) — change to http.get<AgencyWithTotal[]>(...) with no map unwrap; update api.service.spec.ts expectations. The other 7 ApiService methods already consume raw — no change. verify2.sh grep guard ensures no ApiResponse string survives. CRITICAL: backend + frontend must land in the same deploy (Render auto-deploys main), otherwise /api/agencies returns [] shape while an old frontend still reads .data.

stories.title: Reconcile usa-spending.service.spec with rate-limited impl via fake timers
stories.goal: Make sync/usa-spending.service.spec.ts green against the current rate-limited implementation. The suite drifted when the rate limiter was added (commit 907fa4a): fetchWithRetry/rateLimit use real setTimeout (RATE_LIMIT_MS=1000 plus exponential backoff), so tests that exercise fetchSpendingByAgency (which loops 5 AWARD_TYPES, each awaiting the rate limiter) exceed Jest's 5s timeout and report partial call counts. The fetch calls themselves are already mocked (jest.spyOn(global,'fetch')) but unconfigured calls fall through to the real network and the timer waits are real. Goal: zero real-time waiting and zero live-network calls, with the existing call-count assertions (which already account for the 5-award-type loop, e.g. retry-thrice expects 3 attempts) passing.

acceptanceCriteria.text: usa-spending.service suite is green (no timeouts, no live network)
full backend suite green at 207/207 (incl. usa-spending)

stories.notes: Pre-existing test-infra drift (independent of E19). Root cause: the rate limiter added in 907fa4a (RATE_LIMIT_MS=1000 via real setTimeout) + exponential backoff in fetchWithRetry means every fetchSpendingByAgency call waits real wall-clock time across its 5 AWARD_TYPES loop -> Jest 5s timeout -> partial call counts (e.g. the 'HTTP 500 thrice' test reports 5 calls instead of 3 because it times out mid-loop). Fix with fake timers: call jest.useFakeTimers() in beforeEach and jest.useRealTimers() in afterEach, and flush the queued timers with jest.advanceTimersByTimeAsync(ms) after kicking off each awaited service call (advance enough to cover rate-limit + backoff, e.g. 60000ms). Pattern for a success test: const p = svc.fetchSpendingByAgency({...}); await jest.advanceTimersByTimeAsync(60000); const result = await p; expect(...). For a rejects test: const p = svc.fetchSpendingByAgency({...}); await jest.advanceTimersByTimeAsync(60000); await expect(p).rejects.toThrow(). ALSO add a default fetch mock so unconfigured calls return an empty 200 page instead of calling through to the real api.usaspending.gov: jest.spyOn(global,'fetch').mockImplementation(() => Promise.resolve(new Response(JSON.stringify({results:[],page_metadata:{has_next:false}}), {status:200, headers:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}}))) (mockImplementation, not mockResolvedValue, so each call gets a FRESH Response body). The retry-table expectedAttempts already encode the 5-award-type loop (6 = 2 retry + 4 single-fetch; 3 = throw-on-first-award-type), so once timers are fake + network is mocked the existing assertions should hold. Do NOT change usa-spending.service.ts impl.

stories.title: Angular polish: stable chart-input references + functional geography paginator + modern control flow
stories.goal: Close the polish gaps surfaced by the Angular deep-architecture review. The frontend is structurally sound post-E18 (deep ApiService, no premature stores), but three view-layer items remain: (1) inline object/array literals in chart input bindings make ng2-charts re-render on every change-detection cycle; (2) the geographic-view mat-paginator is decorative (no page handler, table renders all rows); (3) mixed legacy *ngIf/*ngFor vs modern @if/@for control flow across components. All three are behavior-preserving polish with an existing in-repo pattern to copy. No backend changes.

acceptanceCriteria.text: no inline object/array literal in any chart [datasets] or [data] binding (stable computed references)
dashboard chart options are a stable field (not an inline template literal)
geographic-view paginator is functional: binds a paged source and wires a (page) handler
no legacy structural directives (*ngIf/*ngFor) in the polished components

stories.notes:
Behavior-preserving view-layer polish (see Angular deep-arch review). Three independent fixes, each with an existing in-repo pattern to copy:

(1) STABLE CHART-INPUT REFERENCES. ng2-charts BaseChartDirective re-renders whenever its [data]/[datasets]/[options] input REFERENCE changes. Inline literals in the template create a NEW object/array on every change-detection cycle, so the chart redraws on every CD (HTTP completion, tab clicks, selects). Offenders: disaster-lens.component.html binds [datasets]="[{ label: 'Obligated Amount', data: top15Datasets() }]" (new array each CD); dashboard.component.html binds [data]="{ labels: chartLabels(), datasets: chartDatasets() }" and [options]="{...}" (new objects each CD). FIX: hoist to computed/readonly fields in the component. COPY THE EXISTING PATTERN: agency-spotlight.component.ts already does 'readonly chartData = computed<ChartData>(() => ...)' and binds [datasets]="chartData().datasets"; geographic-view.component.ts binds [datasets]="chartData()" (a computed). For disaster-lens add 'readonly top15ChartDatasets = computed<ChartDataset[]>(() => [{ label: 'Obligated Amount', data: this.top15Datasets() }])' and bind it. For dashboard add 'readonly chartData = computed(() => ({ labels: this.chartLabels(), datasets: this.chartDatasets() }))' and a 'readonly chartOptions = { responsive: true, maintainAspectRatio: false, plugins: { legend: { display: false } } }' field (options are static so a plain readonly field is fine — no need for computed), then bind [data]="chartData()" [options]="chartOptions".

(2) GEOGRAPHIC-VIEW PAGINATOR. geographic-view.component.html has <mat-paginator [length]="paginator.length" ...> with NO (page) handler, and the table binds [dataSource]="allStates()" (all rows) — so the paginator is dead UI. FIX: wire paging. COPY disaster-lens: add pageIndex/pageSize signals + an onPage handler + a pagedStates computed (sortedStates().slice(start, start+pageSize)), bind the table [dataSource]="pagedStates()" and the paginator [length]="allStates().length" (page)="onPage($event)". Update the spec to assert paging state.

(3) MODERN CONTROL FLOW. disaster-lens.component.html and geographic-view.component.html still use legacy *ngFor/*ngIf; agency-spotlight already uses @for/@if. Convert *ngFor -> @for (with track) and *ngIf -> @if for consistency. Same rendered output, so specs that query DOM are unaffected.

Do NOT touch ApiService, interceptors, theme/loading services, or any backend. The chart re-render fix is the highest-value item (real CPU saving on interaction); the other two are consistency. Tests are table-driven (AGENTS.md Skill 2); the existing chart/paginator specs need only minimal updates (the dashboard chart spec already reads component.chartLabels()/chartDatasets() — keep those computeds, just add the wrapping chartData).

resolutions.contradiction: File tree shows sync/sync.controller.ts but Step 8 CLI command uses --no-controller for sync module
resolutions.resolution: Do NOT create sync/sync.controller.ts in Epic 1. Only create sync.module.ts and sync.service.ts.
resolutions.reason: CLI command is the executable spec. Sync API endpoints belong to Epic 2. The sync module in Epic 1 is infrastructure only.

resolutions.contradiction: File tree shows common/api-response.interface.ts but ApiResponse<T> already exists in shared/interfaces/index.ts
resolutions.resolution: Do NOT create common/api-response.interface.ts. Import ApiResponse<T> from @shared/interfaces in the interceptor.
resolutions.reason: Duplicating the interface creates drift risk. shared/interfaces/ exists for cross-layer type sharing.

resolutions.contradiction: Epic Step 5 says to possibly exclude ../shared from tsconfig.build.json
resolutions.resolution: Do NOT exclude ../shared from tsconfig.build.json. TypeScript needs to resolve @shared/* types for compilation. These are type-only imports and do not produce output in dist/.
resolutions.reason: Excluding ../shared would prevent tsc from resolving @shared/* imports at compile time, causing build failures.

resolutions.contradiction: Entity glob pattern __dirname + '/../**/*.entity{.ts,.js}' appears to go up from config/ — is this correct?
resolutions.resolution: The glob is correct as written. At runtime, typeorm.config.ts is compiled to dist/config/typeorm.config.js, so __dirname is dist/config/. Going ../ reaches dist/. The glob dist/**/*.entity.js matches all compiled entities.
resolutions.reason: Traced compilation path: src/config/ compiles to dist/config/, __dirname = dist/config/, ../ = dist/, glob matches dist/agencies/agency.entity.js etc.

resolutions.contradiction: data/ directory does not exist and is not tracked by git; better-sqlite3 will fail if parent directory is missing
resolutions.resolution: The TypeORM config factory must call mkdirSync('data', { recursive: true }) before returning the SQLite config object.
resolutions.reason: better-sqlite3 does not create parent directories. The config factory is the correct place to ensure the directory exists before the database file is created.

resolutions.contradiction: GeoSpendingSnapshot unique constraint (stateCode, fiscalYear, agencyId, scope) includes nullable agencyId; SQLite treats NULLs as distinct in UNIQUE constraints, allowing duplicate rows with NULL agencyId
resolutions.resolution: Use the standard @Unique decorator as-is. Accept that SQLite allows multiple NULL agencyId rows with the same (stateCode, fiscalYear, scope). Deduplication is enforced at application level in Epic 2's sync upsert logic.
resolutions.reason: TypeORM does not natively support partial unique indexes. Using a sentinel value would change the data model's semantic contract. Application-level enforcement is standard practice for nullable unique columns in SQLite.

resolutions.contradiction: perCapita fields (GeoSpendingSnapshot, DisasterFundingRecord) are monetary-derived; unclear if stored as cents/person integer or dollars/person float
resolutions.resolution: Store perCapita as integer (cents per person), consistent with the 'all money values are integers' rule. Frontend divides by 100 for display.
resolutions.reason: perCapita = obligatedAmount(cents) / population. Storing as integer cents/person avoids floating-point issues and maintains consistency with the all-money-is-cents convention.

resolutions.contradiction: recoveryRatio (DisasterRecoveryRatio) is a dimensionless ratio (fedSpending/fema), not a monetary amount — should it be integer or float?
resolutions.resolution: Store recoveryRatio as float/decimal type. It is a ratio, not a monetary value. The 'money as cents integer' rule does not apply.
resolutions.reason: recoveryRatio = fedSpendingObligated / femaObligated produces values like 2.5. Forcing to integer would lose precision on a non-monetary derived field.

resolutions.contradiction: File tree shows empty dto/ directories but git does not track empty directories
resolutions.resolution: Create each dto/ directory with a .gitkeep file so git tracks the directory. These are placeholders for Epic 3 DTOs.
resolutions.reason: File tree explicitly shows these directories. Creating them now prevents 'directory not found' issues when Epic 3 adds DTO files.

resolutions.contradiction: Full TDD mandate vs scaffolding stories (S01 install deps, S02 tsconfig) that have no behavior to test
resolutions.resolution: Every story still ships a spec. For scaffolding stories, the spec asserts structural invariants: AppModule compiles and is decorated (S01), @shared/interfaces resolves at runtime (S02). The spec shape is table-driven where there is branching logic; for structural assertions a flat testTable with one row per invariant is acceptable.
resolutions.reason: Keeping a spec in every story is non-negotiable per AGENTS.md Skill 2. Structural specs catch scaffold drift (missing deps, broken aliases) that would otherwise surface mid-build.

resolutions.contradiction: E4-S01 scaffolded @angular/material@^22 + @angular/cdk@^22 against @angular/core@^20 — Material v22 requires Angular v22 APIs, breaking the build. Also @angular/animations was missing (needed by provideAnimationsAsync).
resolutions.resolution: Material/CDK must match Angular core major version. Use @angular/material@^20.0.0 + @angular/cdk@^20.0.0 (latest 20.x LTS = 20.2.14). @angular/animations@^20.3.0 is a required dependency (provideAnimationsAsync lazy-loads it).
resolutions.reason: Material/CDK lag behind core in minor versions — 20.3.x does not exist for Material. Scope-leakage in E4-S04 (builder fixed package.json in worktree but it was not in scope.files) shipped a broken build to main. Caught by QA.
